label: Aliquot
comment: A sample taken from an extract sample for chemical analysis or other treatment.

label: Analysis
comment: Any analysis performed over a sample.

label: Anatomical entity
comment: An anatomical entity can be an organism part (e.g. brain, blood, liver and so on) or a material anatomical entity such as a cell.

label: Bioassay result
comment: An object to store bioactivity results.

label: Bioassay analysis
comment: A bioassay is an analytical method to determine the concentration or potency of a substance by its effect on living animals or plants (in vivo), or on living cells or tissues(in vitro).

label: biological sex
comment: An organismal quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's ability to undergo sexual reproduction in order to differentiate the individuals or types involved [source: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000047]. We recommend to use PATO ontology terms for inviduals of this class.

label: Blank sample
comment: A blank sample.

label: GNPS consensus spectra pair
comment: A pair of 2 GNPSConsensusSpectra.

label: Chemical
comment: A chemical. Recommendation: the instances of this class are ideally Wikidata chemical entries. If it is not a Wikidata entry we recommend to be as much as possible consistent in the whole dataset. Metadata can also be provided in order to describe the provenance of the instance (a chemical), for example, by stating the property prov-o:wasDerivedFrom (PROV-Ontology).

label: Chemical entity
comment: A chemical entity (chemical structure or class).

label: Chemical structure representation
comment: A representation of one or more chemical structures. This representation can be defined as molecular information encodings, compact encoding representations, chemical formulas and graphical representations.

label: Chemical taxon
comment: As a taxonomy for chemicals such as natural products, we recommend to use the taxonomy derived from NPClassifier (i.e., https://w3id.org/emi/npc).

label: Chemical taxon annotation
comment: This class is used to annotate a spectrum feature with a chemical taxon. Prefer the Chemical structure annotation class, if the annotation is mainly to assign a chemical representation structure as defined in the emi:ChemicalStructure.

label: Computational analysis
comment: A data analysis made with computational resources (e.g., computers, software tools).

label: Continuous trait
comment: A specific quantitative characteristic of an individual such as height and blood pressure.

label: Developmental stage
comment: A developmental stage is spatiotemporal region encompassing some part of the life cycle of an organism, e.g., blastula stage. We recommend to use the developmental stage terms from UBERON of a source or target organism sample in an interaction system such as from GloBI.

label: Discrete trait
comment: A discrete trait structured value. When applicable, value units should be defined as the QUDT Units Vocabulary terms.

label: Extract sample
comment: A natural extract obtained from the processing of a RawMaterial.

label: FBMN component
comment: Feature-based molecular network component.

label: GNPS consensus MS2 spectrum
comment: An MS2 spectrum corresponding to the GNPS consensus spectrum of 1 or more LCMSFeature. Recommendation: instances of this class are ideally built as follows: "https://metabolomics-usi.ucsd.edu/dashinterface/?usi1={id}", e.g.: "https://metabolomics-usi.ucsd.edu/dashinterface/?usi1=mzspec:MassIVE:TASK-773effaad17c4124b9ea9004eaa3f526-spectra/specs_ms.mgf:scan:1028".

label: Habitat
comment: It defines an environment where an emi:Interaction occurs. If applicable, we recommend to use the habitat terms from ENVO or CMECS of an interaction system such as from GloBI.

label: InChIKey
comment: A chemical structure represented by its InChIKey.

label: 2D InChIKey
comment: A collection of chemical structures that share the first 14 characters of their InChIKeys, often returned by mass spectrometry based annotation tools.

label: Information source
comment: The source of information. For example, the source of a given annotation that might be a bioinformatics database or machine learning classification model.

label: Interaction System
comment: An interaction record listed (e.g.,in GloBI database) between Source and Target samples.

label: Interaction type
comment: It represents the kind of interaction between a source and a target organism sample in an interaction system (e.g., has pathogen and allelopath of). We recommed using RO vocabulary terms.

label: LCMS analysis
comment: A liquid chromatography\u2013mass spectrometry (LCMS) analysis in a given ionization mode (pos or neg).

label: Negative LCMS analysis
comment: An LCMS analysis in negative ionization mode (pos or neg).

label: Positive LCMS analysis
comment: An LCMS analysis in positive ionization mode (positive or negative).

label: LCMS individual MS2 spectrum
comment: An LCMS feature from a processed LCMS analysis. Recommendation: instances of this class are ideally built as follows: "https://metabolomics-usi.ucsd.edu/dashinterface/?usi1={id}" (e.g., https://metabolomics-usi.ucsd.edu/dashinterface/?usi1=mzspec:MSV000090090:DBGI_01_04_001_features_ms2_pos.mgf:scan:1).

label: LCMS feature set
comment: A set of Liquid chromatography\u2013mass spectrometry (LCMS) features obtained from the processing of a given LCMS analysis.

label: LCMS feature pair
comment: A pair of LCMSFeature.

label: MS2 spectrum
comment: A fragmentation mass spectrometry (or MS2) spectrum.

label: NonTrait
comment: A non trait structured value. When applicable, value units should be defined as QUDT Units Vocabulary terms.

label: Organism
comment: A living being, such as an animal, a plant, a bacterium, or a fungus., Any individual living (or previously living) being. Example: animal, human being. An organism is associated to a taxon such as "Homo Sapiens", NCBI taxonomy identifier: 9606.

label: Group of organisms
comment: A group of organisms (see emi:Organism). These organims may be grouped by biological sex (emi:BiologicalSex), for example.

label: Physiological stage
comment: A physiological stage is a specific phase or condition of an organism in its life cycle (e.g., viability stage). If applicable, we recommend to use the physiological stage terms from PATO of a source or target organism sample in an interaction system such as from GloBI.

label: Quality control sample
comment: A quality control (QC) sample.

label: Raw material
comment: A raw laboratory biological material, i.e., before extraction.

label: Spec2vec document
comment: Document about improved mass spectral similarity scoring through learning of structural relationships. Spec2vec is a novel spectral similarity score inspired by a natural language processing algorithm -- Word2Vec. Where Word2Vec learns relationships between words in sentences, spec2vec does so for mass fragments and neutral losses in MS/MS spectra. The spectral similarity score is based on spectral embeddings learnt from the fragmental relationships within a large set of spectral data. Analysis and benchmarking of mass spectra similarity measures using GNPS data set.

label: MS2Spectra pair
comment: A pair of two tandem mass spectrometry (MS2 spectra).

label: Spectrum annotation
comment: A spectral annotation.

label: Chemical structure annotation
comment: This class is used to annotate a spectrum feature with a chemical representation structure as defined in the emi:ChemicalStructure. Prefer the  Chemical taxon annotation class, if the annotation is mainly to assign taxa to a spectrum feature.

label: System
comment: Any part of the observable universe. A system is often composed of subsystems, for example, the Earth system contains a biome that contains a population that contains organisms that contains organs and so on.

label: Organism taxon
comment: Organism taxon. In the context of open research data, we recommend the use of Wikidata taxonomy as the classification and nomenclature for all of the organisms.

label: Trait
comment: A specific characteristic of an individual. Traits can be determined by genes, environmental factors or by a combination of both. Traits can be discrete (i.e., qualitative such as color) or continuous (quantitative such as height).

label: Wikidata chemical
comment: A Wikidata chemical entry.

label: has anatomical entity
comment: It can be used to assign an anatomical entity (e.g., body parts) to a source and/or target organism sample in an interaction system such as GloBI. When applicable, we recommend to state UBERON vocabulary anatomical entity terms.

label: has chemical structure representation
comment: Recommendation: prefer the chemical structure representation 2D InChIKey that also represents a collection of InChIKeys (i.e. chemicals) with common attributes. As a result, we can reduce the number of triples and redundacy to represent the same information that are common to many chemicals.

label: has consistency score
comment: An ISDB score.

label: has developmental stage
comment: The source or target organism sample of an interaction system such as GloBI can have a developmental stage.

label: has FBMN component
comment: Feature-based molecular network component.

label: has feature area
comment: Assign a feature area to a MS2 spectrum.

label: has final score
comment: An ISDB score.

label: has first member
comment: The first member of a spectral pair.

label: has GNPS LCMS link
comment: It relates  GNPS LCMS link to a LCMS Analysis.

label: has habitat
comment: It can be used to assign a habitat to an interaction system such as from GloBI. When applicable, we recommend to state ENVO or CMECS vocabulary terms.

label: has InChIKey
comment: It can be used to assign an InChIKey either to a chemical or to an spectrum annotation.

label: has InChIKey2D
comment: It can be used to represent the InChIKey2D of an emi:InChIKey that is chemical structure.

label: has LCMS feature
comment: It relates a LCMS feature set to a feature.

label: has LCMS feature set
comment: It relates a LCMS analysis to a set of features.

label: has Massive DOI.
comment: It assigns a Massive repository DOI to a LCMS Analysis.

label: has pair member
comment: LCMSFeature

label: has parent mass
comment: The parent mass of a LCMS feature.

label: has pathway
has physiological stage
comment: It can be used to assign a physiological stage to a source and/or target organism sample in an interaction system such as GloBI. When applicable, we recommend to state PATO vocabulary anatomical entity terms.

label: hasRawSpectrum
comment: The raw spectrum of a LCMS individual MS2 spectrum. Its value is a tuple of binary tuples (see vann:example).

label: has relative feature area
comment: The relative feature area of a LCMS individual MS2 spectrum.

label: has retention time
comment: The retention time of a LCMS individual MS2 spectrum.

label: has row id
comment: The row id of a LCMS individual MS2 spectrum.

label: has SMILES
comment: The simplified molecular-input line-entry system (SMILES) is a specification in the form of a line notation for describing the structure of chemical species using short ASCII strings.

label: has second member
comment: The second member of a spectral pair.

label: has sex
comment: It can be used to assign a biological sex to a source and/or target organism sample in an interaction system such as GloBI. When applicable, we recommend to state PATO vocabulary biological sex terms.

label: has source
comment: An interaction system has a source sample that interacts with a target one. emi:hasSource should state the origin of an interaction.

label: has spec2vec loss
comment: The Spec2Vec losses of a Spec2Vec document.

label: has spec2vec peak
comment: The Spec2Vec peaks of a Spec2Vec document.

label: has spec2vec value
comment: A mass spectral similarity scoring through learning of structural relationships. Spec2vec is a novel spectral similarity score inspired by a natural language processing algorithm -- Word2Vec. Where Word2Vec learns relationships between words in sentences, spec2vec does so for mass fragments and neutral losses in MS/MS spectra. The spectral similarity score is based on spectral embeddings learnt from the fragmental relationships within a large set of spectral data. Analysis and benchmarking of mass spectra similarity measures using gnps data set.

label: has spectral score
comment: An ISDB score.

label: has spectrum
comment: It assigns a MS2 spectrum to, for instance, a LCMS feature set. The domain is not defined to provide flexibility. For a LCMS feature set prefer to state the  emi:hasLCMSFeature property.

label: has super class
has target
comment: An interaction system has a source sample that interacts with a target one. emi:hasTarget should state the target of an interaction.

label: has taxo score
comment: An ISDB score.

label: has universal spectrum identifier
comment: The universal spectrum identifier provided by GNPS.

label: InChIKey value
comment: The InChIKey data value, that is a textual representation of chemical structures. This value is composed of 27 characters and it is a condensed digital representation of the InChI.

label: InChIKey 2D value
comment: The InChIKey 2D data value, that is a textual representation of chemical structures. This value is composed of the first 14 characters of its corresponding InChIKey of 27 character fixed length.

label: is classified with
comment: It is used to classify any individidual with an external controlled-vocabulary in case rdf:type is not the most suitable solution.

label: is InChIKey2D of
comment: It can be used to assign an InChIKey2D to an InChIKey that represents a chemical structure.

label: is InChIKey of
